protocol.description: Enriches artifact markdown files with Obsidian-compatible frontmatter (tags, aliases, cssclass) and injects wikilinks between related artifacts for knowledge graph navigation.

gate.on_false: EXIT — Obsidian integration disabled, skip all steps

context.input: files_modified — list of artifact files created or updated by the current workflow step
config.obsidian — full obsidian configuration block from global.yaml
config.tag_prefix — namespace prefix for all Obsidian tags (default: "kratos")

step.title: Classify each modified file
step.description: For each file in files_modified that resides under {output_folder}/: 1. Read the file's YAML frontmatter 2. Extract the `template` field to determine artifact type 3. If no `template` field exists, infer type from the file path: - planning-artifacts/ → "planning" - implementation-artifacts/ → "implementation" - test-artifacts/ → "testing" - creative-artifacts/ → "creative" 4. Determine the lifecycle phase from the artifact directory

step.title: Add Obsidian tags to frontmatter
step.description:
Add a `tags` array to the YAML frontmatter. Derive tags from existing fields:
**All artifacts:** - {tag_prefix}/type/{template} — e.g., kratos/type/story, kratos/type/prd - {tag_prefix}/phase/{phase} — e.g., kratos/phase/implementation
**Stories only (template == "story"):** - {tag_prefix}/status/{status} — e.g., kratos/status/backlog - {tag_prefix}/priority/{priority} — e.g., kratos/priority/P1 - {tag_prefix}/risk/{risk} — e.g., kratos/risk/high - {tag_prefix}/sprint/{sprint_id} — only if sprint_id is not null - {tag_prefix}/epic/{epic} — e.g., kratos/epic/E1
**If author field exists:** - {tag_prefix}/agent/{author} — e.g., kratos/agent/Theo
Do NOT duplicate tags that already exist in the frontmatter.

step.title: Add Obsidian aliases to frontmatter
step.description:
Add an `aliases` array to the YAML frontmatter for alternative wikilink targets:
**Stories:** aliases = ["{story_key}", "Story: {story_key} — {title}"] **PRDs:** aliases = ["{product_name} PRD"] **Architecture:** aliases = ["{product_name} Architecture"] **Sprint plans:** aliases = ["Sprint {sprint_id}"] **Epic status:** aliases = ["Epic {epic_key} Status"] **Test plans:** aliases = ["{product_name} Test Plan"] **Reviews:** aliases = ["{review_type} Review: {story_key}"] **All others:** aliases = ["{document title from H1}"]
Do NOT overwrite existing aliases — merge with any that already exist.

step.title: Add cssclass for Obsidian CSS snippets
step.description: Add `cssclass: kratos-{template}` to frontmatter. Examples: kratos-story, kratos-prd, kratos-architecture, kratos-sprint-plan. This allows users to create Obsidian CSS snippets that style different artifact types.

step.title: Inject wikilinks between related artifacts
step.description:
Add [[wikilinks]] to connect related artifacts. Use Obsidian's shortest-path resolution with display text: [[filename|Display Text]].
**Story artifacts:** - Epic reference: replace `> **Epic:** {epic_key}` with `> **Epic:** [[epics-and-stories|{epic_key}]]` - depends_on entries: convert each key to [[{key}-*|{key}]] - blocks entries: convert each key to [[{key}-*|{key}]] - traces_to entries: link requirement IDs back to the PRD
**Architecture artifacts:** - Add backlink: `> **PRD:** [[{prd_filename}|PRD]]`
**Sprint plan artifacts:** - Each committed story key becomes [[{story_filename}|{story_key}]]
**Epic status artifacts:** - Each story row becomes a wikilink to the story file
**Review/test artifacts:** - Link to the story being reviewed: [[{story_filename}|{story_key}]]
**Deployment artifacts:** - Link to sprint plan and included stories
**Add a Related Artifacts section** at the end of the document (before ## Review Gate if present): ``` ## Related Artifacts - [[artifact1|Display Name]] - [[artifact2|Display Name]] ```
RULES: - Never create broken wikilinks — only link to files that exist in the vault - Use glob matching for story files: {story_key}-*.md - If the target file cannot be found, use plain text instead of a wikilink

error-handling.description: If any step fails: 1. Log a warning with the step number and error message 2. Continue to the next file in files_modified 3. Never halt the parent workflow 4. Report a summary of enriched vs skipped files at the end
